results.title: What Is Lattics?
results.content:
Lattics

Blog

Pricing

# What Is Lattics?

A knowledge management and writing app designed around how your brain naturally thinks, connects, and creates.

## The Problem with How We Work Today

If you're a researcher, writer, or knowledge worker, you've likely felt this: your ideas are scattered across files, folders, apps, and tabs. To connect one insight to another, you have to switch contexts — opening a new file, searching through folders, or jumping between browser tabs. Every context switch flushes your working memory and breaks your flow. [...] Traditional tools make this worse. Files are isolated. Folders impose rigid hierarchies. Notes are either too structured (databases) or too loose (plain text). And when it's time to turn your research into a paper or report, you're starting from scratch — copying, pasting, and reorganizing everything manually.

There had to be a better way.

## Why "Brain-like"? A Design Philosophy, Not Just a Feature

Lattics isn't built around file systems or database schemas. It's built around a simple insight: your brain doesn't store knowledge in folders. It stores knowledge as a network of interconnected neurons, where each idea is linked to related ideas, and information is retrieved by association, not by navigating a directory tree. [...] Decades later, that vision remains largely unrealized. Most tools still organize knowledge like filing cabinets — not like brains. Lattics is our attempt to finally build the knowledge management system that Bush and Engelbart imagined: one that works with your mind, not against it.

## Start Thinking in Lattics

Free to start — no account required.

Free download for Windows

Also available for macOS

Or learn more on the pricing page.

Lattics - Brain-like Academic and Creative Writing

results.title: Lattics | Its Function “Sucks”, But The Brain Helps
results.content:
Faced with these three views, I’m not quite sure if I’m operating correctly. If you’ve previously used Obsidian’s Canvas, Heptabase, or Scrintal, you might find Lattics confusing. You won’t clearly know which type of view you “should” be using, or if you are “correctly” using the current view.

## It Helps

Although Lattics may not be an excellent note-taking software, it is actually quite good for writing.

Lattics is particularly suitable for “traditional” people — those who have difficulty understanding new things or are reluctant to change their long-established writing habits using local software like Word.

Lattics still retains the retro love for magazines. In its document styles, it offers three magazine-style options: The Economist, Brooklyn, and Medium. [...] Although they provided a Block, it’s a Block with chaotic logic and incomplete functionality. It appears that the Lattics team hasn’t used popular block-based note-taking software like Notion, Heptabase, Capacities, or Feishu. We are also unsure whether there is anyone in the team who writes frequently.

There are also some minor issues. For example, when we change a line to a title by using “#”, we cannot revert it to plain text by pressing “Backspace” at the beginning of the line, nor can we select the title to transform it back to text. Instead, we can only click the six dots at the beginning of the line and then “convert” it to text. [...] Instead, we have to first drag it to the page and then again click “Quote Full Content” to quote the full content.

Press enter or click to view image in full sizeImage 15

Press enter or click to view image in full sizeImage 16

We can see that all operations during input require the participation of the mouse, and it’s cumbersome. The Lattics team seems to have misunderstandings regarding the logic of paragraphs, text, and blocks, along with the use of the mouse and keyboard input. During writing, this strong logical conflict is palpable, hindering our writing and editing process.

results.content:
Lattics is designed for professionals in highly educated such as writers, art critics, bloggers, university students, scientific researchers, engineers, financial analysts etc, significantly improving the efficiency of writing novels, academic papers, research reports, project reports, and knowledge learning Lattics' design principles follow the divergent thinking and the structured info organization of the human brain, providing an immersive, multi-perspective, multi-dimensional, and interconnected info to stimulate creative efficiency. Users who have used Lattics generally believe that it far surpasses other similar software in both writing efficiency, quantity and quality of their work. Key Features: - Good Design: Excellent modern, minimalist, and stylish feel - AI-Assisted Writing: [...] Honestly, i want to preface what i am about to say with the hope you realize that you are truly on to something BIG here. This application is in desparate need of improvements because it is so clearly a potential game changer if it could just be enhanced to the level of performance it so obviously should be functioning at. The editing features are kind of a nightmare as it's practically impossible to edit just one bit of text without it re-formatting the entire page even if you only select a portion of text i found 8/10 times it would just change the whole body of txt. Which is why the undo/redo buttons need fixing because they don't even serve their intended purpose honestly if something changed and i wanted to simply revert to the previous it wouldn't even acknowledge what the format [...] # Lattics - "Brain-like" Writing

AI spies may expose your privacy during every cloud transmission and data storage. Lattics provides powerful capabilities to ensure your data privacy from breaches:

### Local storage first

All contents saved on your computer, No register or login required. No content uploaded to networks or servers.

local storage first

### No monitor, no track

We believe privacy is a fundamental right. That's why we never monitor or track any of your personal data. Your privacy is yours to keep!

privacy protection

### Encrypted third-party cloud sync

Choose a cloud service you trust. No platform other than yourself can read the data, because it is end-to-end encrypted.

sync writing app
